response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile generalist engineer with genuine software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, authored test tooling) and one directly relevant data point: writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI, an actual IDE. However, the role's core required qualifications are largely absent. There is no Kotlin experience, no Spring or enterprise-JVM-framework experience (Quarkus, Hibernate), and only incidental Java exposure (robotics and an orchestration tool, paired with DLang). The deep JVM understanding and Spring-ecosystem expertise the position demands are not demonstrated. The career center of gravity is JS/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang.
strengths: Authored an IDE component in C/C++ (NI LabWindows/CVI), giving real IDE-internals exposure
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, authored test runner and assertion libraries
Broad, proven track record across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and low-latency high-throughput systems
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only incidental Java; deep JVM understanding not demonstrated
No Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate)
Primary expertise (TypeScript/Node.js/DLang) does not align with the JVM tooling focus
